sections.title: Section II - Free Response
sections.directions: You have the indicated time to answer the following free response questions. It is not enough to answer a question by merely listing facts. You should present a cogent argument based on your critical analysis.

questions.prompt: Which of the following combinations of economic policies would be most effective to correct a severe recession? Taxes Money Supply (A) Increase Increase (B) Increase Decrease (C) Increase No change (D) Decrease Increase (E) Decrease Decrease
questions.explanation: Answer key not available yet for this imported exam.

questions.prompt: Anypointinsideaproductionpossibilitiescurveis(A)betterthanpointsontheproductionpossibilitiescurve(B)allocativelyefficientbuttechnologicallyinefficient(C)associatedwithinefficientuseorunemploymentofsomeresources(D)associatedwithmovementsalongtheproductionpossibilitiescurve(E)associatedwithconstantopportunitycosts

questions.prompt: If nominal gross domestic product in a country is $1,600 and the money supply is $400, what is the velocity of money? (A) 400 (B) 10 (C) 4 (D) 2 (E) 0.5
questions.explanation: Answer key not available yet for this imported exam.

questions.prompt: An increase in which of the following would most likely cause the gross domestic product of a country to decrease in the short run? (A) Government spending (B) Imports (C) Money supply (D) Consumption spending by households (E) Investment spending by domestic firms

questions.prompt: In the short run, which of the following will most likely result if wages in an economy rise faster than workers’ productivity? (A) An increase in the price level (B) An increase in firms’ profits (C) An increase in efficiency in labor-intensive industries (D) A larger increase in property income than in labor income (E) A decrease in import prices

questions.prompt: When the central bank sells government bonds on the open market, which of the following will most likely increase? (A) Bank reserves (B) Price of bonds (C) Money supply (D) Nominal interest rates (E) The required reserve ratio Unauthorized copying or reuse of any part of this page is illegal.

questions.prompt: If one-fourth of a nation’s wheat crop is destroyed by a flood in a given season, then the price of wheat and the quantity sold will change in the short run in which of the following ways? Price Quantity Sold (A) Decrease No change (B) Decrease Increase (C) Increase Decrease (D) Increase Increase (E) No change Increase

questions.prompt: Which of the following is a determinant of the amount of money the commercial banking system can create? (A) The marginal propensity to consume (B) The marginal propensity to save (C) The total number of banks (D) The size of the federal debt (E) The reserve requirement

questions.prompt: The unemployment rate is calculated as (A) the number of people not working divided by the population (B) the number of people not working divided by the number of people working both full-time and part-time (C) the number of people working part-time but actively seeking full-time employment divided by the number of people in the labor force (D) the number of people not working but actively seeking employment divided by the number of people in the labor force (E) the number of people in the labor force divided by the population Unauthorized copying or reuse of any part of this page is illegal.

questions.prompt: Crowding out is most likely to occur with which of the following changes? (A) Decrease in government spending (B) Increase in budget surplus (C) Increase in budget deficit (D) Decrease in the real interest rate (E) Decrease in trade deficit

questions.prompt: <span aria-label="Assume the economy of Country X is operating above its full-employment output level. open parenaclose paren Using a correctly labeled graph of aggregate demand, short-run aggregate supply, and long-run aggregate supply, show the short-run equilibrium, labeling the equilibrium price level as and the equilibrium output as . open parenbclose paren Draw a single correctly labeled graph and show both a short-run and a long-run Phillips curve. Identify a point that could represent the short-run equilibrium in part open parenaclose paren and label it as Z. open parencclose paren Assume that the central bank of Country X wants the economy to be in full-employment equilibrium. What open-market operation should the central bank initiate? open parendclose paren Given your answer in part open parencclose paren, what will be the effect of the central bank’s open-market operation on each of the following in the short run? open pareniclose paren The nominal interest rate open pareniiclose paren Employment. Explain. open pareneclose paren Assume that the real interest rate increases in Country X. Will the international value of Country X’s currency increase, decrease, or remain unchanged on the foreign exchange market? Explain. open parenfclose paren Assume that Country X’s financial account open parenformerly called capital accountclose paren balance is initially zero. Given your answer to part open pareneclose paren, will its financial account balance now be in surplus, be in deficit, or remain at zero? The second line is labeled A D prime, begins above and to the right of line A D at a distance away from the vertical axis and is parallel to line A D. The third line is a horizontal line that begins at the price level of 100 on the vertical axis and is labeled A S.
